message:
Improve checksum generation with per-binary .sha256 files

Enhance the build-releases.sh script to generate individual .sha256 files
for each binary in addition to the combined SHA256SUMS.txt file.

Changes:
- Rename output file from checksums.sha256 to SHA256SUMS.txt (standard name)
- Add hash_file() function with cross-platform support (sha256sum/shasum)
- Generate per-binary .sha256 files (e.g., jfp-linux-x64.sha256)
- Skip .sha256 files when iterating to prevent double-hashing
- Extract just the hash value for individual files, not "hash  filename"

This enables two verification workflows:
1. SHA256SUMS.txt: Verify multiple files at once with "sha256sum -c"
2. Per-file .sha256: Quick single-file verification or CDN checksums

The update-cli command can now fetch checksums from either source,
improving robustness when one format isn't available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -70,8 +70,37 @@ echo ""
 
 # Generate checksums
 echo -e "${BLUE}Generating checksums...${NC}"
-CHECKSUM_FILE="$OUTPUT_DIR/checksums.sha256"
-(cd "$OUTPUT_DIR" && sha256sum jfp-* > checksums.sha256 2>/dev/null || shasum -a 256 jfp-* > checksums.sha256)
+CHECKSUM_FILE="$OUTPUT_DIR/SHA256SUMS.txt"
+
+hash_file() {
+  local file="$1"
+  if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    sha256sum "$file" | awk '{print $1}'
+    return 0
+  fi
+  if command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    shasum -a 256 "$file" | awk '{print $1}'
+    return 0
+  fi
+  echo "No SHA256 tool available (sha256sum/shasum)." >&2
+  return 1
+}
+
+> "$CHECKSUM_FILE"
+
+for file in "$OUTPUT_DIR"/jfp-*; do
+  if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
+    continue
+  fi
+  if [[ "$file" == *.sha256 ]]; then
+    continue
+  fi
+  hash="$(hash_file "$file")" || exit 1
+  base="$(basename "$file")"
+  printf "%s  %s\n" "$hash" "$base" >> "$CHECKSUM_FILE"
+  printf "%s\n" "$hash" > "${file}.sha256"
+done
+
 echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Generated $CHECKSUM_FILE${NC}"
 
 # Show summary
